untranslatable_reason: BARR (Bump-and-Run Reversal Bottom) is a visual chart pattern requiring frying pan shape identification, down-sloping trendline detection, and handle formation analysis. These geometric pattern properties cannot be expressed using only the available technical indicators (moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, pivots, volume ratios). The signal source text is also incomplete (truncated mid-sentence at 'larger handle o'), preventing full rule extraction. Pattern recognition would require custom shape-detection logic or candlestick geometry analysis not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Substituted a basic trend-following condition (price above/below SMA-20) as a placeholder for the actual BARR pattern detection. This is a severe simplification and NOT equivalent to the described pattern. A proper implementation would require custom pattern-matching algorithms to detect: (1) prior downtrend, (2) frying pan curvature, (3) down-sloping trendline, (4) handle formation with resistance, and (5) arithmetic chart scaling verification.
